what_it_does: Proposes evidence-based answers to open channel-memory design questions inferred from Tim's stated preferences and session signals, then records Tim's confirmed decisions and extensions.
observations: Contains a 'get-a-feel' profile distilled from the feedback-* corpus plus the current session.
Frames seven design questions (Q1-Q7) as proposals, each with evidence, confidence level, and a one-line react prompt.
Lists decisions already locked by Tim's direct words before the inferred section.
Maps inferred proposals to lead-owned lanes and notes where the lead confirmed or grounded them.
Records Tim's confirmed decisions (D1-D8) with four new design elements to thread into the build.
Uses many bracketed internal links (e.g., [[the-heart]], [[extraction-vs-judgment]]) as evidence anchors.
standouts: Tim confirmed an extensible multi-embedding registry and multi-scale dimension-aware chunking.
Preference derivation is specified as tool-driven, not static file-read, and modeled as a conceptual hierarchy.
The file dogfoods the capability being designed by inferring preferences from the cross-session memory corpus.
conventions: Heavy use of bracketed internal links to cite evidence.
Confidence labels (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) attached to each proposal.
Structured Q&A format with proposal, evidence, rationale, confidence, and react prompt.
Top metadata block declares trust level, author, date, and method.
Direct in-session decisions recorded in a dedicated section with timestamp and trust label.
concerns: Several proposals are labeled HIGH confidence before Tim's reactions are recorded, which may overstate certainty.
Dense, link-heavy prose may be hard to interpret without the linked corpus.
Some questions (Q4/Q5) are explicitly joint with or owned by the lead, creating cross-file dependencies.
The confirmed-decisions section retroactively validates the inferences, potentially blurring the inferred-vs-direct boundary.
notes: A preference-inference plan that also acts as a decision log, capturing both proposed answers and Tim's confirmed reactions.
questions: What is the exact schema of the fixed, extensible facet registry?
How is the feedback-* corpus structured and kept anti-recency?
What is the precise relationship between a channel manifest and the ~/.vi/rules auto-load mechanism?
Which embedding models and 'ways' will populate the multi-embedding registry?
How does tool-driven preference derivation technically differ from reading static preference files?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to propose inferred preferences for Tim to react to; actual content also serves as a finalized decision log after D1-D8 confirmations, so it slightly exceeds the react-only framing.
apparent_themes: Preference inference from memory rather than recency.
Extraction versus judgment (small models extract, large model judges).
intention_signals: The file is trying to close open design questions by surfacing evidence-based proposals, inviting rapid owner reaction, and then locking the resulting decisions into the build plan.
novelty: It explicitly dogfoods the preference-memory capability it is designing: using the cross-session feedback corpus to infer what Tim would want, then recording his direct corrections as the authoritative design delta.
contribution: A worked example of evidence-linked preference inference plus a compact decision record (D1-D8) that a fused system can use to trace which design choices were proposed, why, and which Tim confirmed.
summary_for_embedding: A plan document that proposes evidence-based answers to open channel-memory design questions, grounded in Tim's preference corpus and session signals, and records his confirmed decisions including a multi-embedding registry, multi-scale chunking, tool-driven preference derivation, and conceptual-hierarchy preference modeling.
intention_for_embedding: To give Tim a reactable slate of inferred design preferences and then lock the confirmed answers into the channel-memory build plan.
